title: Code principles — WSL-first, maintenance-light
principle: Good code reads like clear thought: self-explanatory flow, no tricks, no promises the runtime cannot keep. Beauty is in structure, not cleverness.
wsl_goal: One host, one entry, small surface — no maintenance-heavy rival drivers on Linux.
rules: ONE entry per concern: wsl_conductor.sh, _m1_autoseal.py, run_r0_migration_phase_orchestrator.py
Hardware truth in one module: tools/r0_migration_hw.py — no PowerShell fragments in call sites
No false parallelism: path-sim is serial; do not advertise multi-core speedups where physics forbids it
No fail-open gates: if a lock or seal check fails, say so plainly; never silently skip safety
Delete before abstract: retire rival .py drivers; extract pure helpers only when importers exist
Comments explain why, not what — the what must be readable from names and flow
WSL native path ~/active_alpha_model; cross-platform paths via Path(__file__), never hardcoded E:\\
retire_not_carry_to_wsl: rival autonomous loops (executive, finish_push, watch_loop, autopilot)
consolidation_gate: control/r0_migration/py_consolidation_plan.json — execute on WSL after M1+M2 seal, not before
